reference_answer: Pool Corporation, a Delaware corporation.
Peter D. Arvan.
John B. Watwood.
John E. Stokely, who was previously Chair of the Board.
Melanie M. Hart, Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer.

May 4, 2026 — designated in the filing as the "Transition Effective Time." Mr. Arvan's cessation as President and CEO, his resignation from the Board, and Mr. Watwood's appointment as President and CEO are all effective at that same moment.
Mr. Watwood joined Pool Corporation in January 2026 as executive vice president.
On the Board since 2000; lead independent director since 2003; Chair of the Board since 2017.

The Company's 2026 Proxy Statement, filed with the SEC on March 26, 2026. (The Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q filed on October 29, 2025, is also referenced — but in the Item 9.01 exhibit list, not in the Item 5.02 body.)
The Company will file an amendment to this 8-K containing the information called for by Item 5.02(e) of Form 8-K within four business days after the information is determined or becomes available.
$800,000 per year.
125% of his annual base salary, upon the achievement of certain performance metrics.
Approximately $1,750,000, split fifty percent as restricted shares and fifty percent as performance-based shares.

Mr. Stokely: $50,000 per month while he serves as Executive Chair. Mr. Whalen: $5,000 per month while he serves as interim lead independent director. Both also remain eligible for compensation under the Board's standard compensation policy.
Eight directors. In connection with Mr. Arvan's resignation, the Board reduced its size to eight.
$1,000,000 (125% of $800,000). It is not guaranteed — eligibility is conditioned on achievement of certain performance metrics, and 125% is the target opportunity, not a floor.

At a 50/50 split of approximately $1,750,000, that is approximately $875,000 in restricted shares and approximately $875,000 in performance-based shares. The filing uses the word "approximately" for the total.
Approximately four months — Mr. Watwood joined Pool Corporation as executive vice president in January 2026 and was appointed President and CEO effective May 4, 2026.

Exhibit 99.1 (the press release) is furnished under Item 7.01 and is expressly stated not to be deemed "filed" for Section 18 purposes. Exhibit 10.1 (the Form of Employment Agreement) is incorporated by reference to Exhibit 10.3 of the Company's Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q filed on October 29, 2025.

The 8-K states only that the Board was "reduced … to eight directors" in connection with Mr. Arvan's resignation. Since one director (Mr. Arvan) departed and the new total is eight, the Board had nine directors immediately before the reduction. The filing does not state the prior number explicitly.

No. The 8-K states that information required by Item 5.02(e) of Form 8-K "has not been determined as of the date of this report" and that the Company "intends to file an amendment to this filing containing the information called for by Item 5.02(e) of Form 8-K within four business days after the information is determined or becomes available."
No. The 8-K states: "Mr. Arvan's resignation from the Board is not the result of any disagreement with the Company on any matter relating to the Company's operations, policies or practices."
No. The 8-K states there are no family relationships between either Mr. Watwood or Mr. Stokely and any director or executive officer of the Company.

No. The 8-K expressly states that the information furnished pursuant to Item 7.01 (which includes Exhibit 99.1) "shall not be deemed 'filed' for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended."
No. The cover-page "Emerging growth company" box is shown unchecked (☐).
